description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the ruby:2.6 Docker image with a postgres service and a global before_script that runs `bundle install` and `bundle exec rake db:create`. Define a notify job named dockerhub that runs in the default test stage, triggers on all branches (`only: branches`), and executes `curl http://dockerhub/URL`. Add a test job rspec that also runs in the test stage on branches, runs `rake spec`, and is tagged ruby and postgres. Add a deploy job production that runs on the master branch and any branch matching `/^deploy-.*$/`, executes `cap deploy production` then `cap notify`, uses ruby and mysql tags, and overrides the DB_NAME variable to mysql. Finally, add a deploy job staging that runs on all refs except the stable tag, runs `cap deploy stating`, is tagged ruby and mysql, allows failure for the spinach job (which runs `rake spinach` on all refs except tags and is allowed to fail), and defines KEY1 and KEY2 variables; all jobs inherit the global DB_NAME=postgres unless overridden.
